title: Kyoukai no Melody
synopsis: Kyousuke, who lost his partner Kai in an accident just before their major debut, has distanced himself from music and lived listlessly. But three years after the accident, Kai suddenly appeared before Kyousuke. "Even if you're alive, if you just stop and do nothing, it's the same as being dead, right?" His blunt, unfiltered way of speaking was unchanged from before his death. And his forcefulness made Kyousuke smile against his will. Persuaded by Kai, Kyousuke confronts the world of music once more and rediscovers the joy of creating sound together. But the happy times with Kai wouldn't last forever... (Source: Crunchyroll News, edited)

title: Julie the Wild Rose
synonyms: Famous Roman Music Theater: Julie of the Grass
Kirin Famous Songs Theater: Julie the Wild Rose
synopsis: Eleven-year-old Julie Braun lives in the lush green mountain pastures of Austria's Southern Tyrol. Her parents are killed by Italian soldiers, and she is sent to Vienna to live with her relatives, the Clementes. She befriends cousins Johan and Tanya but has trouble adjusting to life with Uncle Karl and Aunt Klara. As Austria is plunged into World War I, Karl is fired from his job at a glass factory, and Julie's new family is forced into a life of hardship. (Source: animevice.com)

title: Gestalt
synopsis: Gestalt was shot over the course of a year in a Tokyo dormitory. Each day, Ishida would paint on the wall and photograph it using the available light from the window. The 7-minute long film, which is accompanied by haunting organ music composed by J.S. Bach, consists of those several seconds of film Ishida shot daily. (Source: Midnight Eye)
